description:
# Why

`git::git_ensure_user(dir)` reads `git config user.email` and, if that
returns anything non-empty, skips setting a local identity. A value can
come from the developer's global `~/.gitconfig` — so balls' state-branch
commits (`balls: seed state branch`, etc.) silently depend on ambient
global git config being present and readable.

Consequence: in a genuinely HOME-less environment — a minimal CI runner,
a container, a process with HOME unset for any legitimate reason —
`state_repo::ensure` fails its `seed()` commit with:

  Author identity unknown
  *** Please tell me who you are.
  fatal: unable to auto-detect email address (got '<user>@<host>.(none)')

This is the same failure bl-bfa8 chased, but bl-bfa8 only removed the
*test-induced* HOME race (a sibling test's `env::remove_var("HOME")`).
The underlying fragility — balls' own commits depending on the user's
global git config — is still there and would bite a real HOME-less run.

# Scope

Make `state_repo`'s git commits self-sufficient: they should not depend
on ambient/global git identity.

Design question (discuss-first):
- Option A: `git_ensure_user` always sets a *local* user.name/user.email
  on the balls-owned `.balls/state-repo/` clone (it is balls-internal;
  arguably balls should author its own state commits as itself anyway).
  Changes commit authorship: state-branch commits become `balls` instead
  of the developer.
- Option B: pass `-c user.name=... -c user.email=...` on the specific
  commits `state_repo` makes, leaving `git_ensure_user` untouched.
- Either way, do NOT change identity handling for the *project* repo's
  commits — only the balls-owned state clone.

Pick A or B with the maintainer; the authorship change in A is the only
real trade-off.

# Origin

Surfaced reviewing bl-bfa8 (the HOME-race flake fix, main a70997a). The
test race was the trigger that exposed it, but the dependency on global
git config is a standalone latent bug, filed separately so bl-bfa8 could
stay a surgical test-only fix.
title: state_repo::ensure depends on ambient git identity (~/.gitconfig)
